documentation.value: Creates a new named variable and assigns it a value.
The name of the variable to create or overwrite.
The value to assign (string, number, boolean, or another variable).
Captures the rendered content between the tags and assigns it to a variable as a string.
The variable name to store the captured content.
Iterates over an array or range, rendering the enclosed block for each item.
The loop variable name for each element.
The array, hash, or range to iterate over.
Conditionally renders the enclosed block if the condition is truthy.
An expression that evaluates to truthy or falsy.
Renders the enclosed block unless the condition is truthy (opposite of if).
An expression; the block renders when this is falsy.
Creates a switch statement. Renders the block matching the variable's value.
The variable to compare against.
The value to match in each when clause.
Inserts the rendered content of another template file. Deprecated in favor of render.
The filename of the template to include (without extension).
Renders another template with its own isolated scope. Variables can be passed explicitly.
The filename of the template to render (without extension).
Optional variables to pass into the rendered template.
Outputs the enclosed content without parsing any Liquid tags. Useful for displaying Liquid code examples.
Prevents the enclosed content from being rendered or output. Used for inline comments.
Cycles through a group of strings and outputs them in order on each successive call. Commonly used in loops.
The first value in the cycle.
The second value in the cycle.

signatures.label: {% tablerow item in collection cols:n %}...{% endtablerow %}

documentation.value: Generates an HTML table by iterating over a collection. Automatically wraps output in <tr> and <td> tags.
The loop variable for each element.
The array to iterate over.
Optional number of columns per table row.
